post.summary: Process a natural language query via SSE

get.description: List recent conversations for the authenticated user.

schema.title: Response List User Conversations Api Conversations Get

get.description: Get full conversation with all messages.

schema.title: Response Get Conversation Detail Api Conversations  Conversation Id  Get

delete.description: Delete a conversation and its messages.

get.description:
Retrieve a cached schema snapshot for a database configuration.

Returns ``null`` if no cache entry exists or the TTL has expired.

schema.title: Response Get Cached Schema Api Schema Cache  Db Config Id  Get

delete.description: Delete a cached schema snapshot from Redis.

schema.title: Response Delete Cached Schema Api Schema Cache  Db Config Id  Delete

get.description: Return the remaining TTL (seconds) for a cached schema.

schema.title: Response Get Schema Cache Ttl Api Schema Cache  Db Config Id  Ttl Get

post.description:
Store a schema snapshot in Redis.

The ``workspace_id`` from the authenticated user overrides any
value in the request body to enforce tenant isolation.

schema.title: Response Cache Schema Api Schema Cache Post

delete.summary: Invalidate all cached schemas for workspace
delete.description:
Invalidate all cached schema snapshots for the current workspace.

Requires admin role.

schema.title: Response Delete Workspace Cache Api Schema Cache Delete

post.description:
Introspect an external database and discover its schema.

This connects to the specified database, reads table/column metadata,
and caches the result. Use this before generating a vault.

Requires admin role.

Generate Obsidian vault files from a cached schema snapshot.

First call /schema/discover to discover and cache the schema,
then call this endpoint to generate the markdown files.

Requires admin role.

post.summary: Synchronize Vault Markdown files with live DB Schema
post.description:
Sync Vault Markdown schemas by introspecting the live DB.

Only available to admins. Uses the credentials saved in Tenant Config.

post.summary: One-shot: discover schema and generate vault
post.description:
Convenience endpoint that discovers schema and generates vault in one call.

Combines /schema/discover and /vault/generate into a single operation.

Requires admin role.

post.summary: Enrich a single table with metadata
post.description:
Add business metadata to a single table's vault file.

Use this to add:
- Column descriptions
- Business-friendly names
- Keywords for semantic search
- Manual relationships (for tables without FK constraints)

schema.title: Response Enrich Single Table Api Workspaces Vault Enrich Post

post.summary: Bulk enrich vault with JSON payload
post.description:
Bulk enrich multiple tables via JSON payload.

Use this to programmatically add metadata to multiple tables.

Requires admin role.

schema.title: Response Enrich Bulk Json Api Workspaces Vault Enrich Bulk Post

post.description:
Upload an Excel file to enrich the vault with column descriptions.

Expected format: Each sheet is a table name, with:
- Column A: Column name
- Column B: Column description

The Excel file from the database documentation works directly.

Requires admin role.

schema.title: Response Enrich From Excel Upload Api Workspaces Vault Enrich Excel Post

post.summary: Add a manual relationship between tables
post.description:
Add a manual relationship between two tables.

Use this for tables that don't have formal FK constraints
but have logical relationships (e.g., CONTRNO in multiple tables).

schema.title: Response Add Manual Relationship Api Workspaces Vault Enrich Relationship Post

get.summary: List all tables in the vault
get.description:
Get a list of all tables in the workspace vault.

Returns basic info for each table (name, description, column count).
Use GET /vault/tables/{table_name} for full details.

schema.title: Response List Vault Tables Api Workspaces Vault Tables Get

get.description: Get full details for a specific table including all columns and metadata.

patch.description:
Update table and column metadata.

Frontend-friendly endpoint for editing vault content.
Only provided fields are updated (PATCH semantics).

schema.title: Response Update Vault Table Api Workspaces Vault Tables  Table Name  Patch

patch.description:
Update description for a single column.

Simplest endpoint for inline editing in the frontend.

schema.title: Response Update Column Description Api Workspaces Vault Tables  Table Name  Columns  Column Name  Patch

get.description: Return auto-generated sample questions for the workspace schema.

schema.title: Response Get Workspace Suggestions Api Workspaces  Workspace Id  Suggestions Get

post.description: Register a new customer/workspace and admin user.

get.description: Get dashboard stats for the current user.

List all memory entries for the workspace.

Agent memory persistence lives in **Qdrant via Mem0** (LOCKED-DECISIONS #1).

Args:
    memory_type: Filter memories by type (all, user, team, cache)

Returns:
    List of memory entries with id, entity_id, content, type, created_at

parameters.name: memory_type

schema.description: Filter by memory type: all, user, team, or cache
schema.title: Memory Type

parameters.description: Filter by memory type: all, user, team, or cache

schema.title: Response Get All Memories Api Admin Memory Get

delete.description:
Delete a specific memory entry.

Only admin can delete memories.

schema.title: Response Delete Memory Api Admin Memory  Memory Id  Delete

patch.description: Update TTL for a specific memory entry.

schema.title: Response Update Memory Ttl Api Admin Memory  Memory Id  Patch

post.description:
Trigger memory cleanup based on retention policy.

Retention policy:
- QUERY_CACHE: 7 days (default) - stale SQL mappings
- USER: 90 days (default) - old preferences
- TEAM: ∞ (never expires) - institutional knowledge

Only admin can trigger cleanup.

schema.title: Response Cleanup Expired Memories Api Admin Memory Cleanup Post

get.description: Get memory statistics for the workspace.

schema.title: Response Get Memory Stats Api Admin Memory Stats Get

get.description: List all team conventions for a team.

schema.title: Response List Team Memories Api Admin Team Memory Get

post.description: Create a new team convention/business rule.

schema.title: Response Create Team Memory Api Admin Team Memory Post
Response Delete Team Memory Api Admin Team Memory  Memory Id  Delete

get.description: List all teams belonging to the current workspace's customer.

schema.title: Response List Teams Api Admin Teams Get

post.description: Create a new team for the current workspace's customer.

delete.description: Delete a team (must belong to the current workspace's customer).

get.description: List all users belonging to the current workspace's customer.

schema.title: Response List Users Api Admin Users Get

post.description: Create a new user within the current workspace.

patch.description:
Update a user's role and/or team assignment.

Verifies the user belongs to the current workspace's customer.

get.description:
Get tenant limits.

Daily token limit comes from the active TokenQuota (real);
the per-query row limit is a platform default until a per-tenant override exists.
Degrades to defaults if the table is not yet migrated / DB is unavailable.

schema.title: Response Get Tenant Config Api Admin Tenant Get

patch.description:
Update tenant limits.

Only admin can update tenant configuration.

get.description:
List audit logs with filtering and pagination.

Filters (all optional):
* **user_id**    – show logs for a specific user UUID
* **action**     – filter by action name (``query``, ``export``,
                   ``policy_evaluation``, etc.)
* **resource_type** – filter by resource type (``table``, ``query``,
                       ``artifact``, etc.)

Results are ordered newest-first.

schema.title: Response List Audit Logs Api Admin Audit Logs Get

get.description: List all token quotas for the workspace.

schema.title: Response List Quotas Api Admin Tokens Quotas Get

get.description: Get daily token usage records for the workspace.

schema.title: Response Get Token Usage Api Admin Tokens Usage Get

get.description:
Get all vault policies for the current workspace.

Returns every policy scoped to the workspace (both team-specific
and the default / org-wide policy where ``team_id`` is NULL).

schema.title: Response Get Team Policies Api Admin Vault Policies Get

put.description:
Create or update the vault access policy for a team.

*team_id* can be ``"default"`` to target the workspace-wide default
policy (stored with ``team_id == NULL``).

schema.title: Response Update Team Policy Api Admin Vault Policies  Team Id  Put

get.description: Get the CMEK/Encryption configuration for the tenant.

patch.description:
Update CMEK/Encryption configuration.

If switching providers (e.g. app -> aws), in a full implementation this would 
re-wrap the DEK using the new KEK. For MVP, we save the configuration.

get.description: Get high-level metrics for the admin dashboard.
Get detailed health status of all system dependencies.
Unauthenticated health check.
Return the authenticated user's profile and permissions.
Return workspace metadata — user must belong to this workspace.
Admin-only dashboard.
Team management — available to team_lead and admin roles.
Preview generated SQL — requires can_view_sql permission.
Example endpoint that only needs workspace_id, not full user.
